artifacts.content:
Hi Mom,

Finally sitting down for a second now that the kids are in bed and I’m not glued to my laptop for billing. Figured I’d actually write instead of just texting you “we’re fine” every Sunday.

Ethan’s hanging in there, but this fall has been…a lot. We’ve been taking him twice a month since September 2024 to see that child psychologist I told you about, Dr. Karen Patel at Aurora Children’s Behavioral Health Clinic over on South Chambers Road. The visits show up in my email as “Ethan Rodriguez – school anxiety and inattention follow-up,” which pretty much sums it up. Dr. Patel is still sorting out whether it’s generalized anxiety, ADHD-Inattentive Type, or both, but Ethan seems to like her.

You’ll probably roll your eyes, but I had that telehealth follow-up with my own psychiatrist, Dr. Emily Saunders at Rocky Mountain Behavioral Health in Denver, back in July from the dining room table. We ended up increasing my sertraline dose because I told her Ethan’s escalating anxiety and the nightly homework battles were “triggering the same panic I had during my 2016 depressive episode.” Not my favorite sentence to say out loud, but it’s the truth.

I keep hearing your voice from that Sunday call in August, when you said Ethan “sounds just like you did before your breakdown in 2016” and told me to “get him help before it’s too late.” It stung, but you weren’t wrong. That’s part of why I pushed harder to get him in with Dr. Patel and why I actually followed through on the med adjustment with Dr. Saunders.

Day-to-day, it’s the usual chaos on Maple Grove Lane. Mornings are still a mess of snoozed alarms, cereal bowls, Izzy’s missing shoes, and me checking my clinic inbox on my phone. Evenings are a rotation of tacos, pasta, and Ethan melting down over a math worksheet while Carlos gets home dusty from crawling through attics.

He still loves drawing and soccer highlights, and on weekends when he sleeps in till 9 or 10, he’s a different kid—lighter, more talkative. I’m trying to focus on those moments so it doesn’t all feel like crisis mode.

Anyway, I just wanted you to know we really are taking this seriously and that your worrying lit a fire under me, even if I acted annoyed at the time. Call you Sunday like usual.

Love you,
Laura

Hey David,

Thanks again for talking last week. It helped to say some of this out loud to someone who isn’t exhausted from HVAC calls or middle school drama.

We’re still in the middle of figuring things out with Ethan. Since September he’s been seeing that child psychologist, Dr. Karen Patel, twice a month at Aurora Children’s Behavioral Health Clinic on South Chambers Road. The visit summaries literally say “Ethan Rodriguez – school anxiety and inattention follow-up,” which feels weirdly official, but she’s good with him.

On top of that, you know my own stuff has been bubbling up. In July I had a telehealth follow-up with Dr. Emily Saunders at Rocky Mountain Behavioral Health from our dining room table. I told her Ethan’s spiraling at homework time was “triggering the same panic I had during my 2016 depressive episode,” so we bumped up my sertraline. Not thrilled to need more meds, but I also don’t want to white-knuckle my way through another school year.

And then in November 2023, Ethan’s pediatrician, Dr. James Lee at Cherry Creek Pediatrics here in Aurora, put in his chart that we should “consider trial of low-dose SSRI if CBT alone insufficient” after we took him in for those recurring school-related stomachaches and insomnia. I even emailed Carlos the MyChart message titled “Dr. Lee’s note about possible anxiety meds for Ethan” so he’d actually read it instead of just skimming.

So yeah, between Dr. Lee talking about possible meds for Ethan, Dr. Patel working on the therapy side, and Dr. Saunders tweaking my meds, it feels like half our life is spent in or around behavioral health portals.

Meanwhile, the kids are still just…kids. Izzy is obsessed with slime videos and wants to be a “science YouTuber.” Ethan spent all of Sunday sketching dragons and pretending not to care about the Rapids game while secretly checking the score on his phone.

We’re still doing our Sunday night whiteboard routine in the kitchen—writing out tests, appointments, even “soccer at the park” so it’s not all doom and gloom. It helps a little.

Anyway, thanks for being the sibling I can vent to without feeling judged. If you and the Fort Collins crew want to come down one weekend, we’ll bribe you with tacos.

Love,
Laura

metadata.subject: Too many doctors, not enough sleep

artifacts.content:
Dear Mr. Nguyen and Ms. Carter,

I wanted to thank you both for continuing to look out for Ethan and to touch base after the last few weeks.

I really appreciated your email in early October titled “Concerns about Ethan’s focus and missing assignments,” Mr. Nguyen. The details you included about how he asked to sit in the back to avoid being called on and that he “appeared near tears during the 9/30/24 social studies quiz” gave us a much clearer picture of how his anxiety is showing up at school, not just at our kitchen table.

When we met for the Student Support Team meeting on November 7, 2024 in your office, Ms. Carter, I know it wasn’t easy to bring up the possibility that if Ethan’s grades in math and language arts didn’t improve by the end of the fall semester, the school might “recommend summer school for remediation.” I’m glad we could talk about it openly, even though it was hard to hear. Seeing it written in the internal notes under “possible summer school placement – Rodriguez, Ethan” made it feel very real.

I just want you to know that at home we are taking this seriously. We’ve started a Sunday evening routine where we sit at the kitchen table on Maple Grove Lane, go through the upcoming week, and write everything on the big whiteboard—tests, project due dates, even reminders like “charge Chromebook.” We’re also using that color-coded homework folder system you suggested.

Ethan can be very self-critical, and when he’s overwhelmed he says things like “I’m just stupid” or “I hate school,” but he also lights up when he understands something in science or gets positive feedback on his art. Any time you’re able to break tasks into smaller pieces or give him a quick private check-in, it really seems to help.

Thank you again for your patience and for keeping us in the loop. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if there are new concerns, and we’ll do the same on our end.

Best,
Laura Rodriguez
(mom to Ethan, 7th grade)

Jordan: u good? u were quiet in science

Ethan: ya just tired

Jordan: fr or like locker room stuff again?

Ethan: idk

Ethan: remember when those 8th graders were being jerks in Sept

Ethan: after that I sent Malik this long discord msg from my room here on Maple Grove Lane like late at night

Ethan: I literally wrote “Sometimes I wish I could just disappear and not have to go back to that stupid locker room”

Ethan: he even screenshot it and labeled it “Ethan freakout after PE” 😑

Jordan: dang

Jordan: I mean same but also that sounds bad

Ethan: I was just mad and embarrassed

Ethan: I don’t actually wanna like die or anything

Ethan: it’s just easier to say dramatic stuff than “I feel anxious about changing clothes in front of people” lol

Jordan: lol true

Jordan: my mom would probably drag me to like 500 doctors if she saw that screenshot

Ethan: my mom already has me going to this kids place on South Chambers Rd

Ethan: Aurora Children’s Behavioral Health Clinic or something

Ethan: lady there is nice tho we mostly talk about school and how my brain is all over the place

Ethan: kinda

Ethan: better than just sitting in my room thinking about quizzes till 1am

Ethan: yeah come over after lunch

Ethan: we can play fifa and not think about PE or quizzes or whatever

Jordan: bet

Sunday Night Brain Dump – 2024-08-25

Kids finally in bed, dishwasher humming, house on Maple Grove Lane mostly quiet. Trying to get my thoughts straight before another week starts.

Ethan:
- Still waking up with “stomachaches” on test days. Dr. Lee at Cherry Creek Pediatrics (visit back in Nov 2023) wrote “consider trial of low-dose SSRI if CBT alone insufficient” after we took him in for those school-related stomachaches and insomnia. I even forwarded Carlos that MyChart message titled “Dr. Lee’s note about possible anxiety meds for Ethan,” and he just replied with a thumbs-up.
- Since September we’ve been doing twice-a-month sessions with Dr. Patel at Aurora Children’s Behavioral Health Clinic on South Chambers Road. The visit notes literally say “Ethan Rodriguez – school anxiety and inattention follow-up.” Need to remember to print one for my own files.
- Mom (Elena) called from Albuquerque a couple weeks ago and said Ethan “sounds just like you did before your breakdown in 2016” and told me to “get him help before it’s too late.” Hurt to hear it put that way, but it’s probably what pushed me to stop procrastinating.

Me:
- Had the July telehealth with Dr. Saunders at Rocky Mountain Behavioral Health from the dining room table. Upped my sertraline because Ethan’s anxiety and homework battles were “triggering the same panic I had during my 2016 depressive episode.” I hate that phrase but it’s accurate.
- Need to schedule a follow-up with her before school gets too crazy.

Family stuff:
- Carlos admitted during that awful February homework night that he “almost failed out of 9th grade at West Denver High” and that his dad Miguel “used to call me lazy every day.” Explains a lot about how he reacts when Ethan forgets assignments.
- Keep Sunday whiteboard routine going: list tests, soccer, art time so life isn’t all about problems.

Breathe. One week at a time.

metadata.title: Sunday Night Brain Dump – Ethan & me

artifacts.content:
Hey Malik,

It’s Ethan. My mom said it was okay to email you from her laptop since my Chromebook is being weird.

Thanks for not freaking out that night after PE when I spammed you on Discord. I know I said stuff like “I wish I could just disappear and not have to go back to that stupid locker room.” You even screenshot it and called it “Ethan freakout after PE,” which is actually kinda funny now. I was just super mad and embarrassed about those guys calling me too skinny and making fun of my hair.

I don’t actually want to disappear. I just hate that feeling in my chest when I have to change in front of everyone or when there’s a quiz. Mr. Nguyen wrote this email to my mom and the counselor in early October called “Concerns about Ethan’s focus and missing assignments,” and he said I asked to sit in the back so I wouldn’t get called on and that I “appeared near tears during the 9/30/24 social studies quiz.” I didn’t even realize teachers could see that.

Now there’s all this school stuff happening. They had a Student Support Team meeting on November 7, 2024 in the counselor’s office and the assistant principal said if my math and language arts grades don’t get better they might “recommend summer school for remediation.” It’s in some notes as “possible summer school placement – Rodriguez, Ethan.” My parents tried to act chill but I could tell they were stressed.

Also my dad told me once when we were in the kitchen that he “almost failed out of 9th grade at West Denver High” because he skipped classes and didn’t turn in work, and his dad Miguel “used to call me lazy every day.” So I guess school has always been annoying in my family.

Anyway, just wanted to say thanks for being cool about everything and still wanting to play games and soccer and stuff. It makes school less terrible.

See you tomorrow,
Ethan

Title: Check-ins, kids & my brain – November

Notes for myself so I don’t lose track of everything swirling around Ethan and my own head.

Thursday 11/07/2024 – 2:30–3:15 p.m.
Location: Horizon Ridge Middle School, counselor’s office
Event: Student Support Team meeting for Ethan
Attendees: Me (Laura), Carlos, Mr. Nguyen, Ms. Carter (counselor), assistant principal
Notes: This is the meeting where the assistant principal said that if Ethan’s math and language arts grades don’t improve by end of fall semester, they might “recommend summer school for remediation.” It’s written in their internal notes as “possible summer school placement – Rodriguez, Ethan.” Remember how small Ethan looked sitting in the hallway afterward.

Monday 11/18/2024 – 4:00–4:50 p.m.
Location: Aurora Children’s Behavioral Health Clinic, South Chambers Road
Event: “Ethan Rodriguez – school anxiety and inattention follow-up” with Dr. Karen Patel
Notes: Ask about how to handle nights when he says things like “I’m just stupid” and refuses to start homework. Bring up incident where he told Malik on Discord, “Sometimes I wish I could just disappear and not have to go back to that stupid locker room,” which Malik screenshotted as “Ethan freakout after PE.” Clarify safety vs. venting.

Tuesday 11/26/2024 – 12:30–1:00 p.m. (telehealth, dining room table)
Location: Rocky Mountain Behavioral Health (virtual)
Event: Follow-up with my psychiatrist, Dr. Emily Saunders
Notes: Update her that after increasing my sertraline in July (“Medication adjustment – parenting stress”) because Ethan’s anxiety and homework battles were “triggering the same panic I had during my 2016 depressive episode,” I’m sleeping a little better but still feel on edge before school mornings and big meetings.

Ongoing: Keep Sunday night whiteboard routine at 7:30 p.m. in the kitchen on Maple Grove Lane. Small steps.

Hi Mom,

I wanted to send a quick update about my week and some things I need help with. First, I know you’ve been wanting to keep track of everything, so here’s a little rundown:

This week, I have a science project due on Wednesday and a math quiz on Friday. I’ll try to start working on the project right after school tomorrow, but I might need to ask you or Dad for some help with the math stuff. 

I also wanted to let you know that I haven’t met with a mental health professional yet, and we haven’t completed a safety plan. I know it’s something you’ve been thinking about, so I thought I’d remind you.

I’ve been feeling a bit stressed, but I’m trying to stay on top of things. I’ll make sure my assignments are in my backpack (and not just left on my whiteboard this time). I also want to remember to call Grandma Elena this Sunday. I know she’ll want to hear how school is going and about soccer, even though I’m not playing this season.

Also, can you please update my primary contact phone number in my phone list? I have your cell as (303) 555-9184, but I should add your work number too, which is (303) 555-2107. Just in case I need to reach you when you’re at work.

And finally, I wanted to remind you that my birthday is coming up next March 22nd! I can’t believe I’ll be 13 soon. Maybe we can plan something low-key, like a small family gathering? Let’s chat about that soon.

Thanks for all your help. I really appreciate it.

Love,
Ethan

P.S. I double-checked, and our postal code is 80013 in case you need it for anything.
